factors.summary: The company's organic revenue growth is experiencing mixed effects due to the integration of Exelis. While the integration has contributed to revenue increases in certain segments, it has also impacted the overall organic growth.

evidence.text: Our acquisition of Exelis on May 29, 2015 significantly impacted our operating results, as further discussed in the remaining sections of this MD&A.

The increases in segment revenue, gross margin, ESA expenses and operating income in the second quarter of fiscal 2016 compared with the second quarter of fiscal 2015 were primarily due to the inclusion in segment operating results of Exelis operations...

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed effects from Exelis integration suggest a neutral sentiment regarding organic growth.

factors.summary: The acquisition of Exelis has generally had a positive impact on the company's operations, with successful integration leading to revenue and margin improvements across multiple segments. However, the Highlights section acknowledges that the acquisition also posed integration challenges, indicating a mixed outcome overall.

evidence.text: Our acquisition of Exelis on May 29, 2015 significantly impacted our operating results, as further discussed in the remaining sections of this MD&A.

The increases in segment revenue, gross margin, ESA expenses and operating income in the second quarter of fiscal 2016 compared with the second quarter of fiscal 2015 were primarily due to the inclusion in segment operating results of Exelis operations...

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The acquisition had both positive and negative impacts, leading to a neutral assessment.

Anticipated losses on development and production contracts or programs in progress are charged to operating income when identified. factors.summary: The company is implementing restructuring charges and cost reduction initiatives across multiple segments, which have contributed to both favorable and unfavorable adjustments in operating income. Cost savings from the Exelis acquisition are being realized, leading to margin improvements in some segments, while other segments are facing increased costs due to restructuring charges and impairment estimates.

evidence.text: In the second quarter of fiscal 2016, we incurred charges for restructuring and other items of $17 million, $6 million, and $12 million in our Communication Systems, Electronic Systems and Critical Networks segments, respectively.

Estimate at completion adjustments had the following impacts to operating income for the periods presented:...Favorable adjustments$49$32$96$64 Unfavorable adjustments(25)(16)(55)(26) Net operating income adjustments$24$16$41$38
The decrease in segment ESA percentage in the second quarter of fiscal 2016 compared with the second quarter of fiscal 2015 was primarily due to cost savings realized after our acquisition of Exelis.

The increase in segment ESA percentage in the second quarter of fiscal 2016 compared with the second quarter of fiscal 2015 was primarily due to the mix of programs requiring a higher ESA expense and charges for restructuring and other items.

The increases in segment ESA expenses and ESA percentage in the second quarter of fiscal 2016 compared with the second quarter of fiscal 2015 were primarily due to recording in the second quarter of fiscal 2016 a preliminary estimate of $367 million for a non-cash charge for impairment of goodwill and other assets...

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed cost impacts from restructuring and savings.

factors.summary: The company's margin trajectory shows mixed trends. While gross margins have increased, the gross margin percentage has decreased due to factors like a shift in contract mix towards lower-margin cost-plus contracts and asset write-downs. However, operating margins have shown stability or slight increases across several segments, attributed to cost savings and improved program execution.

evidence.text: The increase in gross margin in the second quarter of fiscal 2016 compared with the second quarter of fiscal 2015 was primarily due to the inclusion in our operating results of gross margin from Exelis operations... The decrease in gross margin as a percentage of total revenue... was primarily due to a shift in the mix of contract types, toward an increased percentage of lower-margin cost-plus contracts, lower gross margin percentage in Exelis legacy tactical radio and night vision product lines and write-downs of certain assets related to restructuring programs.

Estimate at completion adjustments had the following impacts to operating income for the periods presented:...Favorable adjustments$49$32$96$64 Unfavorable adjustments(25)(16)(55)(26) Net operating income adjustments$24$16$41$38

The slight decrease in segment operating margin percentage in the second quarter of fiscal 2016 compared with the second quarter of fiscal 2015 reflected the items discussed above regarding this segment.
The decrease in segment operating margin percentage in the second quarter of fiscal 2016 compared with the second quarter of fiscal 2015 reflected the items discussed above regarding this segment.
The increase in segment gross margin percentage in the second quarter of fiscal 2016 compared with the second quarter of fiscal 2015 was primarily due to improved program execution.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed trends in margins due to both increases and decreases.

factors.summary: The company's debt leverage has remained stable, as evidenced by its maintained credit facility and long-term debt levels. However, there is an increase in net interest expense due to higher debt levels from financing activities related to the Exelis acquisition.

evidence.text: The increase in net interest expense in the second quarter of fiscal 2016 compared with the second quarter of fiscal 2015 was primarily due to higher overall debt levels as a result of our issuance of $2.4 billion of new debt securities and our borrowing of $1.3 billion under a new term loan agreement to finance our acquisition of Exelis in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2015.

We were in compliance with the covenants in the 2015 Credit Agreement at January 1, 2016, including the covenant requiring that we not permit our ratio of consolidated total indebtedness to total capital, each as defined in the 2015 Credit Agreement, to be greater than 0.675 to 1.00 from and including May 29, 2015 until and including February 29, 2016 and 0.65 to 1.00 thereafter.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Increased net interest expense due to higher debt from acquisition.
